intake.context: ω-cycle #5 caught macOS-silent-bypass: BSD `date(1)` does not support %3N format; emits literal '...N' suffix while exiting 0, so `||` fallbacks never fire. to_int() rejects → catch sets v=0 → freshness guard collapsed. Carry-forward: sweep all hexa tools across nexus/hexa-lang/hive for the same pattern.

tier_1_findings.claim: On macOS, the `date +%s%3N || python3 ...` and `date +%s%N` shellout pattern silently collapses to 0 in 20+ source-file sites across nexus/hexa-lang. BSD date emits literal '...N' string AND exits 0, so OR-fallbacks never fire and to_int catches → v=0. The bug surfaces wherever the result drives a comparison (freshness, elapsed, cost) — every such site silently bypassed on macOS until this audit.

Correct cascade is THREE explicit steps with `.contains('N') == false` guard between BSD-date attempt and fallback. Two existing patterns prove this: hive/core/util/lockfile.hexa (gold-standard with last-char check + python3 + date+%s) and stdlib/portable_fs.hexa (`!s.contains('N')` guard). Both pre-existed the bug discovery — the fix template was already canonical, just not propagated.

5 sites under R37/AN13/L3-PY policy (cli/blowup/, sim_bridge/anu_time/) cannot use python3 fallback. For these, cascade reduces to: BSD date+%s%N (with N-guard) → date+%s padded with 9 zeros. Loses sub-second resolution but no longer collapses to 0.

fixed_silent_bypass_with_cascade.before: 'date +%s%3N || date +%s%N || echo 0' + ms<1e6 guard (too loose for BSD partial-parse)
fixed_silent_bypass_with_cascade.after: BSD N-guard + tightened threshold from 1e6 to 1e12 (epoch-ms in 2026 is ~1.78e12)
fixed_silent_bypass_with_cascade.consequence_pre_fix: guard only caught literal 0; if sf() returned 1776797833 (partial parse stripping N), guard bypassed with ms ≪ 1e6 false

fixed_silent_bypass_with_cascade.before: embedded awk `now_ms()` does `(L+0)/1000000` on BSD '...N' → wrong scale (returns sec instead of ms, off by 1000x)
fixed_silent_bypass_with_cascade.after: awk function checks `L ~ /N$/` → falls back to `date +%s * 1000`
fixed_silent_bypass_with_cascade.consequence_pre_fix: phase-profile rel_ms reported as raw seconds, all phase durations off by 1000x

fixed_silent_bypass_with_cascade.before: `t0=$(date +%s%N); ... t1=$(date +%s%N); echo $((t1-t0))` — bash arithmetic on '1776...N' fails or returns 0
fixed_silent_bypass_with_cascade.after: case match on '*N' in shell, fall to python3 time.time_ns() per t0/t1
fixed_silent_bypass_with_cascade.consequence_pre_fix: ai_native_bench reports 0ns for every invocation on macOS

unfixable_or_no_impact_excluded.reason: raw-bash .sh — outside hexa-only edit scope; pattern uses `cut -c1-13 || date +%s000` which on BSD: `1776...N` → cut takes first 13 chars `1776797833...` (still digits) → works correctly. Mark as already-safe-by-cut.

raw-bash .sh — has `|| python3 -c '...time.time_ns()'` fallback; BSD output non-empty exit-0 means fallback never fires → silent-bypass risk in .sh. EXCLUDED from this audit per raw#8 hexa-only edit policy. Filed as separate carry-forward.
